title: Docker runtime does not pass Lobster media gateway flags into Aries
page: local Docker runtime at http://localhost:3000 and Lobster Stage 4 media generation
symptom: The running Aries container had OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL, OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN, and OPENCLAW_SESSION_KEY present, but LOBSTER_MEDIA_GATEWAY_ENABLED, LOBSTER_VIDEO_RENDER_ENABLED, and gateway model override variables were missing. That means image/video/text-QA media paths can still use direct provider fallbacks or stay disabled even when the OpenClaw gateway is reachable.
acceptance_criteria: docker-compose.yml passes LOBSTER_MEDIA_GATEWAY_ENABLED into the aries-app service without enabling it by default.
docker-compose.yml passes LOBSTER_VIDEO_RENDER_ENABLED and image/video gateway model override variables into the aries-app service.
docker-compose.local.yml passes the same media gateway variables into both aries-app and aries-app-dev.
.env.example and DOCKER.md explain that OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL/TOKEN only configure reachability and LOBSTER_MEDIA_GATEWAY_ENABLED=1 is the separate opt-in for Stage 4 media delegation.
Compose YAML parses cleanly and the Lobster media gateway test suite still passes.
notes: Fixed in this QA pass by adding safe env passthrough and docs. No secrets are stored in this story.
